records.review_notes: No optical, camera, colorimetric, or spectral calibration procedure is defined.
No camera spectral-sensitivity, radiometric-response, exposure, or device-calibration model is provided.
The source audits previously acquired images and does not prescribe a new capture protocol.
Melanin, hemoglobin, and other chromophores are not modeled or estimated.
Illumination variation appears as a possible source of visual duplication or segmentation difficulty, but no illuminant model or controlled-light assumption is defined.
The study analyzes existing image datasets and does not specify a canonical acquisition instrument or camera system.
The study is a dataset-quality and benchmark audit. The source does not define a light-tissue physical model.
Optical scattering is not modeled or estimated.
Melanin, hemoglobin, and other skin chromophores are not represented.
The source does not define, estimate, or recover scene illumination or illuminant spectral power distribution.
Tissue or surface scattering is not modeled.
The study uses general natural-scene hyperspectral datasets and does not define a skin-specific representation.
No consumer camera, camera spectral sensitivity, camera-response curve, or camera-calibration assumption is involved.
The study does not define a physical light–tissue, reflectance, absorption, or scattering model.
The study does not model or estimate optical scattering.
No color, camera, illuminant, spectral, geometric, or cross-repository calibration procedure is reported.
Camera spectral sensitivity, image signal processing, white balance, exposure, and device response are not reported or modeled.
The paper does not provide a standardized image capture protocol. It reuses images from multiple open dermatology repositories.
Melanin, hemoglobin, and other chromophores are not measured or modeled.
Illumination source, intensity, spectrum, uniformity, and control are not reported.
The source identifies public image repositories but does not establish the cameras, dermatoscopes, imaging devices, or acquisition hardware used to create the images.
The paper does not define a light–tissue, reflectance, absorption, or image-formation model.
Optical scattering is not measured or modeled.
The review discusses pigmentation and erythema visibility but does not estimate melanin, hemoglobin, or other chromophore concentrations.
The review does not introduce a mathematical model, loss function, estimator, or original metric equation.
The review discusses optical visibility and color variation but does not define a formal light–tissue interaction, reflectance, absorption, or scattering model.
Paper 16 is a mini-review and proposed framework. It does not enroll participants, define a patient cohort, or report an original image-level sample.
The review does not define or estimate optical scattering.
